priceSnapshot.note: Consolidating below $66,000 for a 3rd session; +~13% off the Jul-1 $57,750 low. Overnight it absorbed the GOOGL/TSLA after-hours risk-off without breaking the range.

reduceIf.confirmation: 1H close below $65,380 — loses the flip/S1 shelf that held overnight

addIf.confirmation: a sweep into $64,000–$64,100 (value / BB-mid) that reclaims on a 4H close

decisionBox.changed: The feared $65,380 bear trigger from last night's end-day did NOT fire — BTC held the range through the GOOGL/TSLA earnings reaction. Offsetting: Brent ~$95.71 and the US 10Y near a 19-month high keep the $66,000 cap intact.

atr.source: Not verified this run (exchange APIs blocked from sandbox). Prior verified daily ATR(14) was ~$1,553 (~2.36%) on Jul 22 — deliberately NOT carried into any calculation here.

timeframes.structure: Coiling just below the $66,000 cap; no fresh intraday indicator table verifiable this run.
timeframes.source: Intraday indicators not verified (exchange APIs blocked)

timeframes.structure: Capped under $66,000 / $66,114 pivot, holding the $65,380 shelf.
Constructive on paper but capped below $66K — repair-work inside a higher-TF downtrend (daily EMA200 far above).

scalpContext.note: Scalp only the edges — live signals come from the chart, not this report.

etfFlows.note: ~$928M net inflows over 6 sessions ($203.1M on Jul 21, SoSoValue). FXStreet (Jul 23) reports the run approaching $1B across seven sessions — the structural bid remains the bulls' strongest card.

liquidations.note: 24h liquidations ~$30M, aggregate OI ~$48.76B (CoinGlass) — sharply calmer than Jul 22's ~$432M. Long/short split not verified this run.

earnings.note: Beat on Q2 revenue but sank on a 2026 capex hike (AI-spend concern).
Held BTC treasury unchanged at 11,509 BTC; booked a $112M impairment.

indices.note: Closed -0.51% Jul 22 (AH 704.78); mid-range for the month, RSI mid-40s.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: Closed -0.12% Jul 22 (AH 685.15); sitting near the top of its monthly range.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: StockAnalysis price feed is STALE — last verified close $99.24 on May 27, 2026. No current-session print verifiable, so price/changePct are null and dcaSignal is omitted (data-integrity rule). Context: Nikkei was at record highs in late May on the AI-chip frenzy.

divergences: BTC held its range overnight despite GOOGL and TSLA both -4% after hours and a soft Nasdaq close — crypto did not price-discover the risk-off to the downside. A mild positive divergence, tempered by Brent >$95 and a firm 10Y.

calibration.rollingRecord: Range modal correct for a 4th consecutive report; overnight bear trigger did not fire.
calibration.note: Jul-22 end-day flagged a 'Range one 1H close from Bearish' with the break at $65,380. Overnight BTC held ~$65,700+, so the reduce/bear trigger never fired — pre-emptively reducing would have sold a hold that stayed in range, so acting would NOT have helped.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETFs approach $1B in seven-session inflow run (FXStreet)
Bitcoin ETF inflows hit $930M as BTC tops $65K (The Coin Republic)
BTC price prediction as ETF inflows, F&G jump (Invezz, Jul 22)
